where_new_wins: The NEW's signature device -- the slow-burn declarative paragraph built on parallel anaphora -- is its strongest prose engine and has no equal in OLD. 'Take him out and the structure does not sag. It comes down.' The two-beat collapse (long clause, then a hard two-word sentence) is genuinely well-tuned music the OLD never attempts.

Controlled repetition used as rhythm rather than tic: 'His family was here. His children were here. The whole life he had built was on this side of a line...' and the tether refrain ('the way a tether is tied to a stake... so long as it did not break... breaking it'). This is incremental-repetition-as-music (GIPE register), distinct from rule-of-three filler.

The lease arithmetic is rendered as a rhetorical crescendo in NEW: 'going nowhere, generating nothing, becoming no one.' That triad earns its place because each term escalates ontologically (place -> output -> self); contrast OLD's flatter 'going nowhere, generating nothing' equivalent is absent -- OLD renders the same hours as a bald list ('Time enough to learn a language. Time enough to learn a musical instrument...').

The single-sentence paragraph as structural punctuation is deployed with more discipline in NEW: 'I did not understand what I was agreeing to.' / 'It had to do with a lease.' / 'The deepest entry is that it cost me my marriage.' Each lands as a turn. OLD uses the device too ('Sleep took me. By then I had stopped choosing it.') but less often and less load-bearingly.

The wind-tunnel passage is more musical and more thematically integrated in NEW: 'Build it true. Build it true enough that the truth can be seen before it is too late to do anything about it.' The imperative-fragment cadence enacts the discipline it describes. OLD's version ('The discipline was specific. His work had to function in spaceflight...') is correct but inert by comparison.

NEW's close, even pre-sharpening, has a cleaner cadence in the 'pricing is new / seeing is old' chiasmus: 'What is new here is only the pricing of the commons. The pricing is new. The seeing is old.' The OLD's analogous lines ('The pricing is new; the move is old.') are nearly identical but NEW's standalone-sentence setting gives them more air.

Dash hygiene: NEW uses em-dashes very sparingly (close to zero in the body), leaning on commas and periods -- exactly the calibration the project's em-dash-overuse memo asks for. OLD leans on em-dashes heavily ('first flights out of Savannah leave at five forty-five... -- never leave the night before'; 'half here, half pulled toward'; 'Pou it is'; the Dunbar/Du Bois dash pair), several of them doing comma-work.
where_old_wins: OLD opens on a concrete, sensory scene -- 'The sun came up sometime after we cleared the cloud line' -- and sustains physical specificity (the steam-era leather grip, fifty-eight countries, garment bag in the overhead). NEW opens on an abstraction ('The most credentialed person at a company I once worked for made about half of what I made') and asks the reader to 'sit with that,' which is throat-clearing. OLD trusts the image; NEW announces the importance of its own sentence.

OLD's scene-rendering is alive where NEW summarizes. The chore-and-rug scene ('We swept the dust under a corner of the rug. We pushed the sticks from the yard under the old shed... We ran to the rug with a broom and a dustpan') is fully dramatized, with kid-dialogue ('Wait. Wait. Don't get up yet'). NEW compresses the father's discipline into reported abstraction ('the question did the work without an answer') and loses the body of it.

The 'Pou' naming scene is one of the best-written passages in either draft and exists ONLY in OLD: the nephew pointing, 'Pappou' collapsing to 'Pou,' the father's 'particular kind of smile, and something in his eye that I did not at the time understand,' closing on 'The boy was small. He did not know what he had given my father back.' That is earned emotion through restraint. NEW flattens the nickname to a single throwaway clause ('The family called him Pooh, after the bear... a thing my father did not tell me until I was already grown').

OLD's NICU material gives the chapter a beating physical heart -- 'I held him with one hand on his back and the other under his lower body, in case he tried to roll, and he never tried to roll. He settled in immediately and did not move' -- and the breath-count detail ('seven on the inhale or eleven on the inhale'). NEW has no equivalent sensory anchor for the son; the son appears only as abstraction at the close ('somewhere in the world now').

OLD's father-in-the-Appalachians passage has a quiet, true turn: 'the best part of any trip is the journey... It was not until we had stopped hunting that I understood. He was talking about getting to spend time with me.' The delayed comprehension is dramatized, not asserted. NEW carries the father's voice as paraphrase only.

OLD's prose is more varied in sentence length at the micro level; NEW occasionally runs long compound-cumulative sentences that, while controlled, start to feel like a single rhetorical gear -- e.g., 'So he came to work. He did the work, and he did it superbly, and the company collected the difference... and that difference was large, and it went somewhere, and it did not go to him.' Effective once; NEW reaches for the polysyndeton-cumulative move many times and it becomes a recognizable cadence.

OLD's CEO/cable-station scene keeps grounding detail NEW trims ('three and a half hours, then half an hour'; 'a decade-old machine from a famous broadcasting company'; the IMF/World Bank golf-course transition). The texture makes the competence-anecdote feel reported rather than argued.
old_worth_folding_in: The 'Pou'/'Pappou' naming scene in full -- recomposed, not grafted. It is the single richest piece of restraint-emotion in either draft and the NEW currently has nothing in its register that does the grandfather's tenderness. Recompose so it lands inside NEW's quieter, framework-free voice (the nephew, the collapse to 'Pou,' the father's wordless smile, 'what he had given my father back').

The dramatized chore-and-rug scene as the vehicle for the father's 'will you be proud of what I am about to see?' discipline. NEW asserts the discipline; OLD enacts it with broom, dustpan, sticks under the shed, the kids standing taller. Fold the enactment back in so the inheritance is shown, not reported.

The Appalachian-hunting 'the best part of any trip is the journey' beat with its delayed comprehension ('He was talking about getting to spend time with me'). NEW's father is all aphorism-about-work; this beat gives him a second, tenderer register and seeds the absence theme early.

At least one concrete NICU sensory anchor for the son -- the one-hand-on-the-back / 'he never tried to roll' detail, or the breath-count. NEW's son is an abstraction until the final 'somewhere in the world now'; a single held-infant image earlier would make the closing loss land far harder.

The father's 'speaks a hair below the room's volume' passage ('You don't have to exercise power over the others in the room. They quiet themselves down'). It is a vivid, specific, transferable observation about authority that NEW drops entirely, and it characterizes the father as more than a source of maxims.
new_problems: Opening throat-clearing: 'I want you to sit with that the way I had to sit with it, because the sentence is easy to read and slow to understand.' This is meta-commentary about the prose's own effect -- the exact move the OLD avoids by just showing the sun and the grip. (Distinct from the queued opener-enrichment note; even enriched, the 'sit with that' instruction-to-reader should go.)

Recurring meta-narration of the writer's intent: 'I want to be precise about this, because it is the whole reason I am telling you any of this at all'; 'That is the moment I want to mark'; 'I am going fast on purpose. I am going fast because...'; 'I want to bring this all the way home now.' These are tells -- the prose narrating its own structure. OLD almost never does this. It reads as essay-scaffolding leaking into the chapter.

The 'weather' metaphor is overworked: value 'settled, as a kind of weather, into accounts'; 'floats away and settles as someone else's weather'; 'settled... like the weather settles.' Used three-plus times it stops being an image and becomes a tic.

Some near-rule-of-three reflexes survive: 'the years of a constrained life, the knowledge that he could not leave, all of that settled'; 'the hours, and the lives, and the freedom, and the value' (four-beat, but same cadence); 'It did not bring back my marriage and it has not... brought back my son.' Watch the cumulative triad-as-default.
'pennies on the dollar' immediately after 'paid a fraction of what he was worth' is redundant doubling -- two idioms for one idea in one sentence.

The tether metaphor, while strong, is explained one beat too long: 'the way a tether is tied to a stake. If he left, the tether came with him only so long as it did not break, and leaving... risked breaking it.' The literalized unpacking slightly over-narrates an image that had already landed.

Faithfulness-of-the-model lineage close ('a faithful model in the moving air, telling us the truth') is lovely but the chapter rings the wind-tunnel bell so many times by the end ('Build it true,' 'faithful models so the truth could be seen in time,' 'a faithful model in the moving air') that the final chime is slightly pre-spent.
verdict: Split, and it does not resolve cleanly in one direction -- but on pure line-by-line PROSE & VOICE the OLD is currently the stronger READ, by a modest margin, because it shows where the NEW tells. OLD's dramatized scenes (Pou, the rug, the held infant, the Appalachian drives) generate emotion through restraint and concrete sensory detail; NEW too often narrates its own argumentative intent ('I want you to sit with that,' 'I am going fast on purpose,' 'the moment I want to mark') and leans on a single cumulative-polysyndeton cadence plus an over-worked 'weather' metaphor. HOWEVER, NEW has the higher prose CEILING and the cleaner instrument: its anaphoric declarative engine ('Take him out and the structure does not sag. It comes down.'), its disciplined single-sentence turns, its near-zero em-dash count, and the 'Build it true' cadence are better sentence-craft than anything in OLD, and they are uniform in voice in a way OLD (with its essayistic '...Time enough to fall out of love' and dash-heavy texture) is not. The decisive read: OLD wins the chapter you would publish today; NEW wins the chapter you would WANT to publish once its meta-throat-clearing is excised and OLD's three or four dramatized scenes are recomposed into NEW's superior cadence. For this lens, today, OLD edges it -- but the gap is closeable and the NEW is the better foundation to build the final on.

where_new_wins: OPENING GRAB — decisive win. The NEW opens on a single declarative provocation: 'The most credentialed person at a company I once worked for made about half of what I made.' This is a hook in the truest sense — a paradox the reader cannot resolve without reading on, planted in one sentence. It is immediately followed by the instruction 'I want you to sit with that the way I had to sit with it, because the sentence is easy to read and slow to understand,' which converts the reader from spectator to participant in paragraph one. The OLD, by contrast, opens with logistics: 'The first flights out of Savannah leave at five forty-five, and I had taken so many of them by then that I had a discipline about it' — a paragraph of airline-scheduling craft before any stake appears. The author's own complaint that the OLD 'opened slowly' is correct and the NEW is the answer to it.

DISCOVERY IS STRUCTURALLY ENACTED, not cataloged. The NEW has an explicit accelerando movement — the section beginning 'And then I saw it everywhere' literally performs the recognition cascade: watermen → slavery/Tubman → the generic worker, each arriving 'fast and without permission.' The meta-line 'I am going fast on purpose. I am going fast because that is how it actually happened, once the lens had formed' tells the reader the form IS the experience. The OLD never accelerates; it proceeds at one elegiac tempo from plane to grandfather to father to NIH to commute to NICU. It is a beautiful procession, but a procession, not a quickening.

THE NORTH STAR IS HIT EXPLICITLY AND EARNED. The NEW turns the recognition outward onto the reader: 'as you read this... your own examples have started to arrive too. The job you took or left. The person you watched carry something that was never theirs to carry... You have witnessed this your whole life. So had I. The seeing is not new.' This is the book's thesis-feeling — see what you've always witnessed but never named — stated as a direct address that recruits the reader's own memory. The OLD gestures at this only abstractly in its final section ('a way of seeing the architecture clearly enough to name what is being absorbed') and never reaches into the reader's own life.

THE 'TETHER' / 'NO WORD FOR IT' SEQUENCE is the NEW's emotional engine and has no OLD equivalent. 'I did not have a word for what I was looking at... the language was a lie, not because anyone was lying, but because the language had no room in it for a tether' dramatizes the precise gap the book exists to fill — the experience of seeing something real that your vocabulary cannot hold. This is the single most on-thesis passage in either draft, and it is the NEW's spine.

THE CLOSE LANDS HARDER AND CLEANER. The NEW braids three losses into a rising sequence — money ('four hundred thousand dollars... still could not... cover the thing a life will eventually need'), then marriage ('the deepest entry is that it cost me my marriage'), then the son, with the coined turn 'this is the ledger; the numbers are yours to enter.' The structure climbs to 'some entries, fully and honestly made, still cannot be repaid.' The OLD's close is quieter and more dispersed — the son section is set off as a stark one-line stanza ('We have no contact.') which is powerful, but then the chapter continues into framework-explanation and ends on 'I had work to do,' a deliberate flatline that lands as resignation rather than as the reader being handed something.

READER-AS-PARTICIPANT throughout. The NEW repeatedly conscripts the reader: 'Do the arithmetic with me, because the arithmetic is the thing'; 'I will let you sit with what that means for everyone earning beneath the ceiling.' The OLD almost never breaks the fourth wall; the reader watches the author's life rather than being invited to run their own ledger.
where_old_wins: SENSORY IMMERSION AND SCENE. The OLD is far richer in lived, filmable detail — 'the colored light of the NICU,' 'I held him with one hand on his back and the other under his lower body, in case he tried to roll, and he never tried to roll,' the counted breaths ('seven on the inhale or eleven on the inhale'). The NICU-holding scene ('Those hours were the most present I have ever been in my life') is the most emotionally devastating passage in EITHER draft, and the NEW has nothing comparable — it summarizes the son's loss rather than rendering a single held moment.

THE PÒU/POOH RECOGNITION SCENE. The OLD's nephew-naming scene — the boy reaching for 'Pappou' and producing 'Pou,' the father's 'particular kind of smile, and something in his eye that I did not at the time understand,' 'Pou it is' — is a small perfect short story about inheritance arriving unbidden across generations. The NEW reduces Pooh to a parenthetical etymology ('a thing my father did not tell me until I was already grown') and loses the entire emotional event. This is the OLD's best single scene and the NEW's most regrettable cut.

THE DITCH-DIGGING / RUG-AND-STICKS SCENE. The OLD dramatizes the father's discipline-by-question with a full scene: the kids sweeping dust under the rug, the father pausing mid-rise — 'when I go check the work, will you be proud of what I am about to see?' — and the children scrambling, ending 'we were standing taller.' The NEW compresses this to reported summary ('he had a way of asking... the question did the work without an answer'). The OLD lets the reader FEEL the inheritance; the NEW tells them about it.

THE FATHER'S 'JUST BELOW THE ROOM'S VOLUME' PASSAGE. The OLD's account of the naval-architect father who 'spoke a hair below' the room's volume so that 'they quiet themselves down' is a vivid, transferable image of inherited power-without-force. The NEW drops it entirely. It is character texture the NEW's faster pace sacrificed.

PLANE AS RECURRING STRUCTURAL FRAME. Whatever its slow start, the OLD's plane-window frame ('half here, half pulled toward something I cannot quite see the edges of') gives the chapter a returning camera position and a daydreamer's associative logic that motivates the time-jumps. The closing callback — 'The plane was past the cloud line. The sun was up... I had work to do' — completes a frame. The NEW, opening on the colleague by ratified decision, is more thesis-driven but loses this circular, dreamlike containment.

THE APPALACHIAN HUNTING MEMORY. 'The best part of any trip is the journey' — and the slow-dawning realization that the father meant the time with his son, not the scenery — is one of the OLD's quietest, most affecting beats, and it rhymes structurally with the NICU material (presence vs. destination). The NEW has no equivalent father-tenderness scene; its father is rendered through aphorism rather than a remembered morning in the dark.
old_worth_folding_in: The Pòu/Pooh nephew-naming recognition scene — recomposed, not grafted. The NEW already carries a 'recognition arrives before words' motif (the colleague 'on sight,' the inherited bone-sense). The nephew producing 'Pou' and the father's wordless 'Pou it is' is the perfect dramatized instance of inheritance arriving unbidden — it belongs in the NEW's grandfather section as a SCENE, replacing the current parenthetical etymology, because it does emotionally what the NEW currently only asserts.

The NICU held-baby scene's single most concrete image — holding him 'in case he tried to roll, and he never tried to roll,' and the counted breaths — recomposed into the NEW's close. The NEW tells us the marriage and son are 'the deepest entry' but never lets the reader hold the child for one rendered moment. One sentence of that physical presence would convert the NEW's close from argued to felt.

The ditch-digging / rug-and-sticks 'will you be proud of what I am about to see?' as a brief enacted beat inside the NEW's father paragraph. The NEW summarizes the discipline; even two sentences of the children scrambling to redo the work would let the inheritance theme land in the body rather than the gloss.

The Appalachian pre-dawn hunting memory and 'the best part of any trip is the journey' — recomposed as the NEW's missing father-tenderness anchor. It rhymes with the NEW's presence/cost theme and would warm a father who is currently all aphorism. (Fold the realization, not the hunting logistics.)

The father's 'a hair below the room's volume / they quiet themselves down' image — a compact, vivid inheritance-of-quiet-authority detail the NEW could absorb in a sentence to give the father physical specificity.
new_problems: MIDDLE-SECTION SAG UNDERMINES THE PROMISED ACCELERANDO. The NEW promises 'I am going fast on purpose,' but the NIH/CEO/cable-station stretch is its LONGEST and most discursive movement — the cable-station rescue runs to a single ~250-word sentence-pile. After the genuine quickening of 'And then I saw it everywhere,' the chapter decelerates into career anecdote, so the form contradicts its own stated tempo for several pages before the close re-accelerates. The discovery-cascade energy is spent too early and not sustained.

THE 'NO WORD FOR IT' MOVE IS SLIGHTLY KNEECAPPED BY THE FRAMEWORK-FREE CONSTRAINT (known/queued, not penalized as fundamental). The NEW leans hard on 'there was no place... where the thing I was looking at could be set down and named,' which is thrilling — but because it can never name the apparatus or say 'cost severance,' the payoff is a gesture ('this is the ledger; the numbers are yours to enter') rather than the handing-over of an actual instrument. The reader is told a word exists and then, by design, not given it here. This is the right design choice for Ch 1, but it does leave a faint promissory-note feeling at the close.

OPENER IS ABSTRACT-FIRST WHERE THE OLD IS IMAGE-FIRST. The colleague is described almost entirely in metaphor — 'a tether tied to a stake,' 'a roof leans on a single load-bearing wall,' 'sat in my chest like a stone' — but we never SEE him: no face, no desk, no exchanged word, no room. The grab is intellectual and strong, but the colleague remains a figure in an argument rather than a person. (The queued junior/degree-less-narrator enrichment will sharpen the paradox but won't by itself make the colleague visible — consider one concrete sensory anchor for him.)

REPETITION OF THE CORE FORMULA RISKS INCANTATION FATIGUE. 'the value went up and out and settled... as someone else's weather' and near-identical 'value generated by one person and captured by another' / 'floats away and settles as someone else's weather' recur many times. On first hit it's a powerful refrain; by the fourth it edges toward a tic the reader starts to anticipate, slightly dulling the recognition it's meant to trigger.

THE CLOSE STACKS TWO 'DEEPEST ENTRY' CLAIMS. 'But money is not the deepest entry on this ledger' → 'The deepest entry is that it cost me my marriage' → then the son material. The superlative is used twice in close succession, which competes with itself; the marriage-as-deepest and the son-as-final-silence both want to be the floor, and the rapid double-bottom slightly diffuses the single gut-punch the OLD achieves with the isolated 'We have no contact.'

TUBMAN/SLAVERY WEIGHTS-DISCIPLINE PASSAGE, while ethically necessary and well-handled, is the one place the accelerando stalls into a paragraph of meta-reassurance to the reader ('I need you to trust me on that, here at the start, because the book cannot work if you do not'). It's the right content but it momentarily shifts register from felt-recognition to authorial-contract, briefly breaking the spell of the 'I see it everywhere' rush it sits inside.
verdict: For THIS lens, the NEW wins decisively — call it 70/30. The author's specific complaint (slow open) is exactly where the NEW is strongest: it grabs in sentence one with a genuine paradox and immediately conscripts the reader ('sit with that the way I had to sit with it'), where the OLD spends a full paragraph on airline logistics before any stake lands. More importantly, the NEW is the only one of the two that actually ENACTS discovery — the 'And then I saw it everywhere' cascade performs accelerating recognition and then turns it outward onto the reader's own life ('You have witnessed this your whole life'), which is precisely the north star: make the reader SEE what they've witnessed but never named. The OLD, for all its superior scene-craft, is a moving procession at one tempo that never quickens and never reaches into the reader. The NEW's win is structural and thesis-level; the OLD's wins are textural and scenic. But those textural wins are real and recoverable: the NICU held-baby moment, the Pòu naming scene, and the rug-and-sticks beat are the most emotionally alive passages in either draft, and the NEW currently argues feelings the OLD lets the reader hold. The path forward is not to choose — it is the NEW's spine (paradox opener, no-word-for-it, accelerando, reader-as-ledger-keeper) with two or three of the OLD's scenes recomposed back into the body and the close, plus a fix for the mid-chapter career sag that betrays the NEW's own 'I am going fast on purpose' promise.

where_new_wins: FOUR-MOVEMENT STRUCTURE IS GENUINELY STRONGER AS ARGUMENT. The new draft's spine -- colleague (uncounted cost on someone else) -> commute/lease (the same cost in the narrator's OWN life, the 'hinge') -> 'And then I saw it everywhere' (the widening: watermen, slavery, the worker) -> grandfather/father inheritance -> NIH/CEO price-of-the-skill -> family/marriage limit -> close -- builds a single accumulating case. The old draft's plane-frame loops the reader back to the NICU repeatedly but never escalates the CONCEPT; it is a memoir braid, not an argument with a hinge. The new draft earns its thesis ('the deepest costs are exactly the ones that cannot be billed back') where the old only gestures at it.

THE LEASE/COMMUTE IS PROMOTED FROM ANECDOTE TO STRUCTURAL HINGE. In the old draft the commute math sits buried mid-chapter as one more bill-of-particulars beat ('It would be years before I had a name for what the lease had actually cost me'). The new draft makes it the explicit pivot: 'That is the moment I want to mark, because it is small and it is the hinge of everything that follows... I started to look for the missing number everywhere. Not as a theory. As a flinch. The way you favor a joint you have hurt.' That is a better-built engine.

THE NO-VILLAIN MOVE IS STATED AS A PRINCIPLE, NOT JUST AN INSTANCE. New: 'Nobody did this to me. There was no villain at the leasing office... I did it to myself, freely, with full information about everything except the one thing that turned out to matter most.' The old draft has the no-villain beat only at the employer ('There was no villain in it'); the new draft generalizes it and repeats it as a structural refrain (leasing office, then employer, then 'There almost never is'), which is load-bearing for the book's framework-free persuasion of a center-right reader.

THE SLAVERY WEIGHTS-DISCIPLINE PASSAGE IS A REAL GAIN AND IS ABSENT FROM THE OLD DRAFT. New: 'The wrongness there is not a missing number on a ledger. It is the ledger torn up and the person who kept it sold... I will be careful for the rest of this book to never let it sit on the same line as a commute or a lease... Naming the rhyme is not the same as making them equal.' This is exactly the honesty-of-weights discipline the project's memory flags as load-bearing, and the old draft simply does not attempt it. Major win for fidelity-to-the-book's-ethics.

THE WIND-TUNNEL / FAITHFUL-MODEL METAPHOR IS RAISED TO THE BOOK'S THESIS. New makes it explicit and recursive: 'He built faithful models so the truth could be seen in time. I am, in my own way, trying to do the same thing in this book, with the same discipline, and I did not plan that. It was handed to me.' The close then rhymes back to it ('while there is still a faithful model in the moving air'). The old draft has the wind-tunnel description but never closes the loop to the author's own method -- the new draft's structure pays it off.

FRAMEWORK-FREE EXECUTION IS CLEAN AND DELIBERATE. The new draft never names the apparatus or uses 'cost severance,' and substitutes vivid plain-language carriers -- 'the missing number,' 'the column nobody writes down,' 'settled, as a kind of weather, into accounts that were not his.' The old draft leans on the bare word 'framework' five-plus times ('the framework in this book,' 'this was the math the framework would later let me put a number on'), which is exactly the apparatus-naming the new draft is designed to avoid. New wins decisively on the brief's framework-free constraint.

THE COLLEAGUE OPENER LANDS THE BOOK'S CENTRAL ABSTRACTION FASTER. 'The most credentialed person at a company I once worked for made about half of what I made... there was nothing he could do about it.' The tether image ('His permission to remain in the country was tied to his job, the way a tether is tied to a stake') gives a concrete, non-autobiographical instance of value-captured-from-the-maker on page one -- before the narrator implicates himself. The old draft opens entirely inside the narrator's own grief, which is moving but makes the universal claim arrive later and softer.
where_old_wins: THE PLANE/NICU FRAME DELIVERS A DRAMATIZED PRESENT-TENSE SCENE THE NEW DRAFT HAS NOTHING TO MATCH. The old draft's 'The plane banked. The flight attendant set down a second cup of coffee. The morning light from the window had moved across the seatback opposite. The laptop in front of me showed an unopened email. The grip was still wedged under the seat.' -- and the held-baby meditation scene ('seven on the inhale or eleven on the inhale... Those hours were the most present I have ever been in my life') -- are the emotional peak of the whole chapter. The new draft REPORTS the marriage-and-son loss; the old draft STAGES it. This is the single biggest thing the new draft loses.

THE FATHER-CHORE SCENE ('Will you be proud of what I am about to see?') IS FULLY DRAMATIZED IN THE OLD AND COMPRESSED TO SUMMARY IN THE NEW. Old: 'We swept the dust under a corner of the rug. We pushed the sticks from the yard under the old shed... Wait. Don't get up yet. Let us just do a quick walk through. We ran to the rug with a broom and a dustpan...' The new draft flattens this to a single reported sentence ('He had a way of asking, before he came to inspect something I had done, whether I would be proud of what he was about to see'). The scene IS the inheritance; summarizing it loses the felt transmission.

THE 'POU' NAMING SCENE IS A COMPLETE, EARNED EMOTIONAL BEAT IN THE OLD, GONE IN THE NEW. Old: the nephew trying to say 'Pappou,' getting 'Pou,' and the father's 'particular kind of smile, and something in his eye... Pou it is, he said... The boy was small. He did not know what he had given my father back.' This ties the grandfather's nickname (Pooh) to a living moment of generational return. The new draft keeps only the bare etymology fact ('The family called him Pooh') and drops the scene entirely. Worth recovering.

THE FATHER'S MEETING-ROOM CRAFT ('spoke a hair below the room's volume') IS A SPECIFIC, USABLE INHERITANCE BEAT THE NEW DROPS. Old: 'He spoke a hair below it, and only when he had something worth saying... You don't have to exercise power over the others in the room. They quiet themselves down.' This is a concrete skill transmitted father-to-son and a Pattern-2 thread the project memory explicitly tracks. The new draft generalizes the father into 'a way of being in the world' and loses this crisp, demonstrable detail.

THE APPALACHIAN HUNTING-DRIVES SCENE AND 'THE BEST PART OF ANY TRIP IS THE JOURNEY' MISREADING ARC. Old: 'He would point out the birds. He would name the trees... the best part of any trip is the journey. When I was young I thought he was talking about the scenery. Later I thought he was offering some kind of Stoic advice... It was not until we had stopped hunting that I understood. He was talking about getting to spend time with me.' This three-stage reinterpretation is one of the chapter's best small structures and is entirely absent from the new draft. (Note: the new draft was right to demote the plane-frame, but this scene need not have gone with it.)

THE GOLF-COURSE / IMF-WORLD BANK PIVOT gives a concrete, slightly wry account of how the narrator's career escalated ('The man who had been bringing me into project after project was on a golf course one weekend, talking with friends from the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank... He called me Monday morning'). The new draft jumps straight from NIH to 'Years later, as a CEO' with no connective tissue. A small loss of texture and credibility-of-trajectory.

OLD KEEPS THE 'McDOWELL COUNTY' SPECIFIC for the coal-miner comparison ('options a coal miner in McDowell County does not have'); the new draft genericizes to 'a coal miner.' The specific place-name is both vivid and ties to the book's later Appalachia material -- a needless loss of concreteness.
old_worth_folding_in: The held-baby NICU meditation scene -- recomposed, not grafted -- into the new draft's family/limit movement. The new draft tells us the marriage 'is the truest measure of the uncounted cost' but never lets us FEEL the presence that was lost. A recomposed version of 'one hand on his back and the other under his lower body... seven on the inhale or eleven on the inhale... Those hours were the most present I have ever been in my life' would give the abstract closing-entry a body. (Caution: in the new draft's universalized register, render it as the one fully-present thing the pattern stole, not as a returning frame-device.)

The father-chore scene ('Will you be proud of what I am about to see?' -> dust under the rug -> the kids re-doing it and 'standing taller'). The new draft already names the question; fold in just enough of the dramatized scene to make the inheritance shown rather than reported. This is the cheapest high-value rescue available.

The 'Pou'/'Pooh' naming-return moment, recomposed. The new draft spends a sentence on the nickname as bare fact; the old draft's nephew-mispronunciation scene turns it into the chapter's quiet thesis about generational return. Even two or three sentences of it would repay the grandfather movement.

The father's 'spoke a hair below the room's volume / they quiet themselves down' craft-detail, folded into the new draft's father paragraph as the one concrete skill (alongside the dig-ditches advice) that the narrator demonstrably inherited and used as a CEO. It also pre-echoes the cable-station 'ask the one question' competence.

The three-stage reinterpretation of 'the best part of any trip is the journey' (scenery -> Stoic advice -> time with me). It is thematically load-bearing for a book about uncounted time and could live in the new draft's family/limit movement without reinstating the plane frame. The new draft has no equivalent meditation on time-as-the-thing-not-counted at the personal-tenderness register; this supplies it.
The McDowell County specificity for the coal-miner line -- a one-word restore that costs nothing and gains concreteness plus forward-linkage to the book's Appalachia chapters.
new_problems: FACTUAL DRIFT FROM CANONICAL SUBSTRATE: the father's passport. New says 'traveled on a diplomatic passport'; the OLD draft and the author's own substrate (research/story-drafts/_session-answers_2026-05-03.md line 90-92: 'high clearance and a political passport') say POLITICAL passport. This is a no-fabrication-discipline issue -- the new draft changed an author-supplied biographical specific. Restore 'political passport.'

POSSIBLE OVER-PRECISION ON THE GRANDFATHER'S WORK: new says he built 'a scale model accurate to a thousandth of an inch' and worked 'until he retired not long before he died.' The 'thousandth of an inch' figure also appears in old, so it is corpus-consistent, but verify it is author-grounded and not a rhetorical invention that has hardened into a 'fact' across drafts -- it reads suspiciously precise for a remembered family detail. Flag for substrate check, not a confirmed error.

INTERNAL-CONSISTENCY WOBBLE ON THE $400K CEILING CLAIM: new asserts 'salary and commission together touched around four hundred thousand dollars... even at that ceiling... it would not have been enough to cover the year and more of surgery and rehabilitation that life eventually required.' This is a strong, specific, checkable financial/medical claim introduced for the first time (the old draft has no $400k figure and no surgery/rehab year). It is rhetorically powerful but needs substrate-grounding before it ships -- it is exactly the kind of newly-introduced documentary specific the no-invented-claims rule guards against. Confirm the figure and the 'year-plus of surgery and rehabilitation' are author-supplied facts.

THE AIR-COMPRESSOR ANECDOTE IS COMPRESSED INTO A SINGLE BREATHLESS SENTENCE in the new draft ('how he stopped by the workshop and noticed the compressor moved... and was told the bits were worn... and how, a week or two later...'). The project memory's v2.0 anchor explicitly flags this anecdote being 'compressed to a one-sentence summary rather than reconstructed as a scene' as a known drift failure mode. The new draft repeats that exact failure. It should be reconstructed as a scene (the old draft's version is the model).

MILD THESIS-STATEMENT BLUNTNESS AT THE CLOSE: 'The way of seeing in this book hands you a ledger. The numbers are yours to enter...' is doing a lot of explicit telling. The brief already flags the coined line for sharpening, so this is queued -- but note the surrounding paragraph ('I can show you where the missing lines go. I can show you the column that nobody writes down...') leans into anaphora that edges toward the rule-of-three LLM-cadence the project's voice-polish pass watches for.

REPETITION OF THE 'settled, as a kind of weather / settles as someone else's weather' image across four movements (colleague, watermen, the worker, the close). It is a good image, but at four-plus reuses it risks tipping from motif into tic. One or two of the instances could vary.
verdict: On this combined fidelity/structure/gain-loss lens the NEW draft is the stronger chapter and by a clear margin -- call it 70/30. Its four-movement architecture (colleague -> lease-hinge -> the widening -> inheritance -> price -> family-limit -> close) is a genuine argument that accumulates toward an earned thesis, where the old plane-frame is an affecting memoir braid that never escalates the concept. The new draft also executes the framework-free constraint cleanly, adds the load-bearing slavery weights-discipline passage the old draft lacks entirely, and pays off the wind-tunnel/faithful-model metaphor as the book's own method. The old draft's advantage is concentrated and real but narrower: it owns dramatized SCENE where the new draft too often reports -- the held-baby NICU meditation, the father-chore 'will you be proud' beat, the 'Pou' naming return, the hunting-drives reinterpretation. Those are rescues, not reasons to prefer the old structure. The right next move is a whole-cloth revision on the new draft's spine that recomposes (not grafts) two or three of those old scenes into the inheritance and family-limit movements to restore felt presence. Two items must be fixed before any ship regardless of structure: the 'diplomatic passport' drift (substrate says political) and substrate-grounding of the newly-introduced $400k-ceiling / year-of-surgery claim. The known-queued items (junior-developer opener enrichment, ledger-line close sharpening) are not counted against the new draft per instruction.
